# Stage 2 Report — Database Schema & Migrations

- **Date:** 2026-06-23 · **Branch:** `build/v1` · **Commits:** `ef9e4e5`, `13ab82c`

## Goal
Implement the full v1.0 database foundation: all 19 tables (per `database-design.md` + ratified
D11–D25), enums, constraints, indexes, an Alembic migration, and tests — verified against Postgres.

## Implemented
- **`aiwip_core.models`** — 19 SQLAlchemy 2.0 typed models on the shared `Base`, 18 native Postgres
  enums, FKs, unique constraints, indexes. Tables: `users, chats, connector_accounts, messages,
  message_attachments, sync_states, sync_runs, assignees, candidates, candidate_messages,
  candidate_assignees, candidate_labels, labels, work_items, work_item_assignees, work_item_labels,
  ai_runs, evaluation_cases, audit_logs`.
- **Decision encodings:** D11 (decision/risk reserved), D13 (image not photo), D14 (retry trigger),
  D15 (attachment status enum), D16 (no work_item_messages — derive), D17 (missing_fields), D19
  (per-row candidate_assignees.confidence), D20 (context_summary), D21 (credentials_ref = reference),
  D22 (entity_type enum), D23 (ai_runs.input_hash + index), D24 (assignees.user_id nullable + unique),
  D25 (work_item_assignees.is_primary).
- **Constraints/indexes:** unique `(chat_id, external_message_id)`; 1:1 `work_items.source_candidate_id`;
  unique `labels.name`, `users.email`, `assignees.user_id`; pair-uniqueness on all 5 join tables;
  indexes on `messages(chat_id, external_message_id, sent_at, processing_status)`, `candidates.status`,
  `ai_runs.input_hash`. `BigInteger` for all Telegram external/user IDs.
- **Alembic harness** in `core/` (`alembic.ini`, `env.py` with `ALEMBIC_DATABASE_URL` override,
  `script.py.mako`) + autogenerated initial migration `a050622bac2d`, hardened to drop enum types on
  downgrade (fully reversible).
- **Tests** (`core/tests/`): constraints (unique message, 1 candidate→1 work_item, D24), relationships
  (candidate→multi-messages #59, candidate→multi-assignees #60, work_item→multi-assignees #69, tags via
  both join tables), priority-nullable, a full seed graph (D16 traceability), and a programmatic
  migration upgrade/downgrade test.

## Not Implemented (intentional)
- `decision`/`risk` candidate types exist as **reserved/inactive** enum members (D11) — never emitted.
- `message_attachments`, `evaluation_cases` tables exist but are exercised by later stages (multimodal /
  evaluation); fine to remain empty for now.

## Tests Run / Results
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` → **18 passed** vs **native Postgres 16** (`aiwip_test`).
- **Migration cycle** (against `aiwip_migrate_test`): clean(0 tables/0 enums) → `upgrade head`(20/18) →
  `downgrade base`(1/0) → `upgrade head`(20/18). Fully reversible and re-runnable.

## Adversarial Review (workflow `stage2-schema-review`, 8 agents)
- 6 dimensions reviewed (enum-fidelity, column-completeness, D11–D25 compliance,
  constraints/indexes/relationships, test-coverage, completeness-critic); each finding adversarially
  verified (refute-by-default). **2 findings; 1 confirmed real, 1 rejected.**
- **No schema deviations found** — enums, columns, decisions, and constraints all matched the canonical
  docs.
- **Confirmed (high):** test-plan #60 "a candidate can have multiple assignees" was untested (the
  existing test only covered `work_item_assignees`). **Fixed** (`13ab82c`): added a dedicated
  candidate-multi-assignee test; re-labeled the work-item test as #69; added a `work_item_labels`
  assertion for parity.
- **Rejected (low):** "no behavioral assertion on `work_item_labels`" — verifier ruled it a non-blocking
  nitpick (schema correct, persistence already exercised); addressed anyway as a cheap parity win.

## Bugs Found / Fixed (during build)
- **Telegram IDs in `Integer`** (int32) → overflow risk → switched external/Telegram IDs to `BigInteger`.
- **Migration not reversible** — `downgrade` dropped tables but left 18 enum types, so re-`upgrade`
  failed with CREATE TYPE conflicts → added explicit `DROP TYPE` to downgrade. Caught by the
  reversibility check, not assumed.

## Remaining Risks
- `docker compose up` still unrun (native brew runtime by choice); container path authored, not executed.
- Alembic autogenerate does not detect future **enum value additions** (e.g. activating `decision`/`risk`)
  — those migrations will need hand-written `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE`. Noted for later stages.

## Decisions Made
- **build-D3:** Native Postgres enums storing member **values** (lowercase tokens) via `values_callable`;
  shared one type instance per enum. Under-specified status fields (`connector_accounts.status`,
  `sync_states.status`, `ai_runs.status`) use `String` rather than fabricated enums.
- **build-D4:** Dedicated test databases `aiwip_test` (ORM, transaction-rollback isolation) and
  `aiwip_migrate_test` (migration up/down); both auto-reset per run.

## Files Changed
`core/src/aiwip_core/models.py` (new), `core/alembic.ini`, `core/alembic/env.py`,
`core/alembic/script.py.mako`, `core/alembic/versions/a050622bac2d_initial_schema.py`,
`core/tests/conftest.py`, `core/tests/test_models.py`, `core/tests/test_migrations.py`.

## Next Recommended Stage
**Stage 3 — Authentication & Roles** (email+password + server-side session; admin/assignee role
enforcement; seed first admin). Depends only on `users` (now present).

## Proceed / Do Not Proceed
**PROCEED to Stage 3.** Schema is complete, migration is clean and reversible, 18 tests pass vs real
Postgres, and an adversarial review found no schema deviations.
